rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARILYN A FERRUCCI REVOCABLE TRUST 2005
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/13/2021. 1) Petition must be amended to include the required schedules and re-noticed. 2) Petition must include mandatory judicial council form GC-400. 3) The Petition alleges the settlement required payment of $100k prior to the sale and another $100k following the sale of the property. The order approving the settlement says the $100k relocation payment to be paid from Marilyn A. Ferrucci Trust is approved. Please explain. It appears the settlement only authorized $100k not $200k. 4) Please explain the $1,371.10 in requested attorney costs. 5) Please file the Seller's Statement for the real property. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 11/29/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MAURICIO ANTONIO VARELA CARIAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The total amount of fees and costs of the former/successor conservators and their former/current attorneys, if approved as prayed, will exceed $146,000. The estate owes significant liabilities to the conservator of the person for her caregiving services rendered since July 2019. The successor conservator of the estate estimates that the liabilities for the services rendered through January 2021 will exceed $338,025. The conservator of the person petitioned for approval of compensation in the total amount of $472,537.50 for her caregiver services through 08/31/2021. As of 01/31/2021, the estate held cash of $1,783,327.59. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

The successor conservator of the estate estimates that the liabilities for the services rendered through January 2021 will exceed $338,025. The conservator of the person petitioned for approval of compensation in the total amount of $472,537.50 for her caregiver services through 08/31/2021. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RODNEY BRUCE MIDDLETON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/6/21. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: No courtesy copies of the petition or proof of service have been received yet by the assigned Examiner as of 10/25/21. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] No proposed order. Proposed orders should not be E-filed. [LRSF 14.7 A.] Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies and must be provided at least five (5) Court days before a hearing. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 14.7 C.] Courtesy copies and a proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/23/21 to avoid another automatic continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SALLY CHRISTINE OWENSBY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Approval Of Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/6/21 (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Under what authority is Timothy Breen (not "Breem") representing the heirs in this settlement? Have they appointed him as their attorney-in-fact to settle the dispute? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address this issue must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/22/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. (Mr. Breen filed his petition on 10/29/19, not 10/20/19. Ms. Barfield was appointed Administrator with Will Annexed, not Administrator or Special Administrator, on 10/19/20, not 12/23/20.) Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE EDGARDO M. TORRES REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Find That Assets Are Property Of Trust
rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required to determine whether to grant the petition and confirm all requested property as trust assets. of particular concern are the life insurance proceeds, the 401k and the POD bank accounts, all of which properly pass to the estate of the decedent, not the trust, and the trustee's claim to the property is through the probate estate, via the pour-over will. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SIGMUND A. FREEMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/6/21. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Courtesy copies of papers filed on 10/22/21 have not yet been received yet by the assigned Examiner. [LRSF 14.7 B.] Additionally, item 3g(1) of the petition should have been answered, not 3g(2). Item 6 of the petition must be completed. The nominations by the named alternate executors were for appointment of a SPECIAL ADMINISTRATOR, not an Administrator with Will Annexed. Was the temporary conservator authorized to waive bond for a proposed personal representative? The same attorney represents both the proposed personal representative and the conservator of the person and estate of the surviving wife, which appears to be a conflict. (The charitable organizations and individuals other than the wife were CONTINGENT beneficiaries, not residual beneficiaries, as the entire estate is to go outright to the wife, who survived the decedent by more than 30 days.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/22/21. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. (The courtesy copy of the "supplemental declaration" filed on 10/22/21 was just received on 10/27/21 but it does not clearly set forth HOW the cousins are related to the decedent. [LRSF 14.18 C.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF OTIS R. DAMSLET
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/6/21 (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proof of publication has been filed as of 10/26/21. No will appears to have been lodged. The copy of the will attached to the petition shows that it is not a self-proving will. Proof of Subscribing Witness must be filed. Item 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered. Item 8 does not include the decedent's mother and her date of death (assuming that she is no longer living). [LRSF 14.18 C. and D.] Item 8 also shows the half-sister as "Catherine" Damsleth, although the testator identified his sister as "Camille" Damsleth. Notice was mailed to the father and stepmother at an address that differs from the one shown in item 8 of the petition. If it appears that property will pass to a citizen of a foreign country, notice shall be given to a recognized diplomatic or consular official of the foreign country maintaining an office in the United States. [Prob. C. Sect. 8113.] (Item 3g(2) of the petition should not be answered when there is a will.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/22/21 . Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
